title: Publish release containing completed remediation
outOfScope: Changing release workflow security controls beyond release inputs.
Execution-plan cleanup reserved for Work Item 8.

sources.reason: Current published release identity and supply-chain contract.
Release workflow source identity and evidence requirements.

riskAssessment.reason: Release metadata is bounded and the workflow remains the evidence authority.

executionDecision.reason: User explicitly authorized release publication after all remediation work items.

preReviewWarnings: Verify exact source commit, tag identity, lockfile, smoke, compatibility, SBOM, provenance, and digests.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Release metadata identifies a new candidate tag v0.5.33 based on v0.5.32.
Release notes describe completed remediation without claiming enterprise certification.
The release workflow remains the authority for final publication evidence.
guidelines: Do not claim publication success without workflow evidence.

intent.problem: The completed remediation needs a new immutable release whose source identity and evidence are verifiable.
constraints: Use the existing release workflow as evidence authority.
Do not publish or claim success if any release gate fails.
intent.rationale: Updating candidate metadata and release notes prepares a reproducible release while preserving workflow fail-closed controls.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Candidate metadata is internally consistent for v0.5.33.
Release workflow evidence remains required for final publication.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized automatic release publication, PR merge, and branch cleanup.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
